paragraphs: Charlotte Mason (1842–1923) was a British educator whose philosophy of education has shaped a quiet revolution in homeschooling. Her core conviction: children are not empty vessels to be filled with information, but full persons deserving a rich, living education.

In a Charlotte Mason home, students read whole, living books rather than textbooks. They narrate — retelling what they have read in their own words — rather than filling in blanks. They spend time outdoors, study great art and music, and encounter ideas worthy of their full attention.

At Delight & Savor, we build on this foundation at the high school level — adding rigorous literary analysis, classical rhetoric, and the Great Conversation with history's greatest thinkers. The result is a student who can read carefully, think deeply, and write with confidence.

quote.text: The question is not, — how much does the youth know? when he has finished his education — but how much does he care?
